surface: REST /api/webhooks (CRUD + test-ping) and the outbound webhook payload
naming_law: public fields use 'call' (BUY|SELL|HOLD), never 'signal'; price_at_call (not price_at_signal)
auth: Authorization: Bearer <api-key>. A non-null key is required to OWN a subscription (keyless-anon / x402 / internal rejected). Universal access — no per-event/asset/endpoint tiering; gated only by the monthly call quota (Free 100 / Starter 3000 / Pro 15000 / Enterprise 100000). Each DELIVERED event draws down the owner's quota.

POST /api/webhooks.secret_exposure: subscription.secret is returned ONCE here only; never on GET

outbound_payload.description: JSON POSTed to the subscriber URL, HMAC-SHA256 signed

forbidden_key_rationale: outcome_*/pfe_*/mae_*/return_pct_*/price_after_* are Phase-E internal (Data Integrity LAW); owner_key is the API key (never echoed); 'signal' violates the NAMING LAW for NEW public fields (use 'call').
cache_contract: none — all responses are dynamic, no-store
consumers: P0-3 per-platform adapters (3commas / cryptohopper / tradingview) — future waves
drift_check_command: K=av_starter_DRIFTCHECK; B=https://api.algovault.com; curl -s -X POST $B/api/webhooks -H "Authorization: Bearer $K" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"url":"https://example.com/hook","events":["trade_call"]}' | jq 'keys, .subscription|keys' ; echo 'assert: no key in subscription matches ^(outcome_|pfe_|mae_|return_pct_|price_after_|owner_key$|signal$)'
notes: verify_url uses the spec hash form (https://algovault.com/verify?hash=<signal_hash>); the public /verify page + the HTTP /api/verify-signal route currently key off integer signalId — a ?hash= handler is filed as a landing follow-up (getSignalByHash + the MCP verify-signal tool already resolve hashes).
